failure_modes.text: Self-justification can turn the conscious self into a press secretary for earlier decisions instead of a seeker of truth.
failure_modes.mitigation: Ask what evidence would cause a reversal and whether anyone has looked for it; the press-secretary instinct defends decisions rather than seeking truth.

failure_modes.text: Bias blind spot can keep people convinced they are less trapped by commitment than everyone else even while their story hardens.
failure_modes.mitigation: Use structured red-team exercises that explicitly challenge the prior commitment; awareness alone does not dissolve the bias.

failure_modes.text: Groupthink and tribal loyalty can make consistency with the group feel safer than updating on reality.
failure_modes.mitigation: Collect individual positions before group discussion to prevent tribal consistency pressure from overriding independent judgment.

premortem_questions.text: What would I do if I had no sunk costs invested in this outcome?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would I do if I had no sunk costs invested in this outcome?

premortem_questions.text: What assumptions underlie the framework I am using?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What assumptions underlie the framework I am using?

premortem_questions.text: What would you have to believe?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

heuristics.text: Treat abandoning a bad idea as learning progress rather than as weakness so reversal becomes psychologically possible.
heuristics.source_quote: making mistakes is okay, but failing to learn from them is unacceptable

heuristics.text: Force an antithesis before the group synthesizes, so loyalty to prior commitment does not silently set the answer.
Assume you are wrong long enough to search for the flaw in the commitment story before defending it.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the intervention layer on reversal-enabling questions, antithesis discipline, and self-justification breaks.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broad influence-technique sprawl because the raw source is strongest on de-locking rigid commitments.
open_questions: The first heuristic compresses a wider raw discussion of learning culture into one action rule against escalation.

allies: The raw source is strong on cognitive dissonance, scaffolding, and purpose alignment.
antagonists: The source explicitly names confirmation-bias, intellectual-humility, and hammer-style misframing pressure.
structured_tensions: No explicit structured tension block was preserved from the raw file.
